sanskrit:
बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम्।
स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते॥
transliteration: bāhya-sparśeṣv asaktātmā vindaty ātmani yat sukham | sa brahma-yoga-yuktātmā sukham akṣayam aśnute ||
literal_meaning: One whose self is unattached to external contacts finds that happiness which is in the self; united in Brahman-yoga, he enjoys imperishable happiness.
contextual_meaning: True, lasting happiness is not found in external stimuli but in a settled, self-referential awareness that is no longer hooked by sensory pull. When attention is no longer colonized by outward objects, an inner reservoir of stable joy (ānanda) is contacted; this is the experiential core of liberation.
psychological_state: Secure autonomous functioning; low reactivity to reward cues; trait mindfulness; intrinsic motivation predominates.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media feels empty; you put the phone down, take a mindful breath, and notice a quiet, steady well-being that doesn’t depend on likes or the next hit of dopamine.
anchor_text: Find joy within, untouched by outer pulls.
